item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow, that was clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it, but I'd like to point out that we have an SCP that's basically the same thing, except it's a camera that shows what the subject of the photograph would rather be doing at that moment. the difference is that that article flat-out states what it's SCP does, so there's no reason that this one shouldn't either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0

Title: File:Pocket microscope.jpg
Author: No machine-readable author provided. Roger McLassus 1951 assumed (based on copyright claims).
Uploaded on: 2005

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the scip, but in my heart, I know I’m just upvotong because of TIME CUBE.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent edit was reverted, as it referenced a Group of Interest that is not on the mainsite, and understand of the entry is hampered by this.

Collaborators, please note that open-entry logs are not the place to advertise your own personal content in place of writing mainsite works. Furthermore, collaborative entries should improve the quality of the log itself, rather than making obscure references to content that isn't really related at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay. This whole article seemed like a throwback to the older collaborative logs, so I just thought it would fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be a straightup madness to test this with SCP-2140 and I can't justify it. I kinda want to write one anyway. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't that funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How about this:

Proposal
hide

Researcher | Date of test: Dr. Eye | 07 - XX-XXXX
Text: The preamble to the United States Constitution, handwritten by Dr. King.
Result: Text reading "Why do we even have to [EXPLETIVE] do this? Its just going to be more [EXPLETIVE] apple seeds, that's what it [EXPLETIVE] always is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just added a cross test with SCP-2203 to the collaboration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's under a collapsible on the first pages of comments. Spoilers below.

Basically, it shows the thoughts of those that wrote something down as they were writing it down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it. There's clearly an internal logic here, but I don't get it.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed it,thanks for telling me! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i am not sure if it is good and that it is in good format.

Yeah, uh… you have an unnecessary space between the first and second lines of the entry, "researcher" is spelled incorrectly, generally blackboxes not expungment is used to censor dates, and you need spaces after the colons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. You didn't immediately show what the real anomalous effect was, you let the reader piece it together through the experiment logs. Awesome use of show don't tell, and some good humor without being lolfoundation. You've got my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry about the rate module can you fix it?

For reference, you could view the source code for previous versions of this article and pick out the source code from them via "History" and then "S" (for source code).

 [[>]] [[module Rate]] [[/>]]

FYI, the [[>]] and [[/>]] aligns the module to the right.

The best way out of this situation is to not tamper with the rating module and just add your piece without ruining it for others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry about the rate module can you fix it?

P.S Hope you like litlle thing that i added but i am not sure if it is good and that it is in good format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Time Cube needs to be a SCP or at least a anomalous theory based off Time Cube.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kek'd at Time Cube. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always had a weakness for SCP-887 and articles like it.

This article is kind of intended as a stylistic homage to the older sort of SCP article that used to get put up a lot back in 2009-2010 or so. This includes the cross-testing.

And I figured that if I'm going to cross-test with SCP-887, I might as well try to say something subtly interesting and profound (rather than just goofy) about 887.

If SCP-2236 shows SCP-887's output as blank, then what _is_ really going on with SCP-887?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Fire away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Implying, furthermore, that someone in the Foundation has a genuine need to research what constitutes suitable housing for a unicorn princess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i mean the tag is still there and spikebrennan hasn't died so

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this still a collaborative?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, once I saw this comment, it occurred to me to reread this to figure out what it actually did and what I missed. And now that I get it… goddamn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how 887's output is "blank".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is now explicitly tagged as a collaboration. But I reserve the right to prune, as do the site mods/senior staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Determining if it created the ideal of what the writer wanted, or just transcribed their basic thoughts was an important test!

Also, gotta know if the cupcake castle is suitable for a unicorn princess. (I imagine that checking this was actually fairly easy with good drawings of the castle and decide architecture rendering software. But unicorn princess!)

Editted because I apparently cannot type today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm…

I slightly dislike the concept. However, the experiment log is excellent. Good choices of tests. Although I think that the experiment log should be on its own page, or at least hidden.

So… No vote.

P.S. There needs to be a -J based off this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could really go for a burger, but don't feel like cooking, maybe I should go out…1

Footnotes
1. Original text: I love this, it feels like the kind of article that really got me into the site to begin with. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not random. It's what the author was thinking when they wrote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edit: I'm an idiot. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you aren't going to scare the hell out of me, then at least show me a good time. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this were a -J, the Time Cube and childish scribbling results would be reversed.

I love this article for its classic feel. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cupcake-building was the ONLY interesting point here. Other than that, it's too obvious, no twist at all for me. No-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly what I was going to comment on this. They took the time to check if it would be stable!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well done, I thought. It dawned on me at 'piss off' what it was doing, and everything from there on was gravy. I very much liked your choices of test material, particularly the wikipedia page.

Damn but I wish I'd had this thing in high school english lit classes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One tiny thing irks me… Why are they using 72-point type to test this thing? If it's a microscope, shouldn't they use the smallest text they can? Reading 72-point text through a microscope sounds like an annoyingly tedious process.

I do like some of the recent additions though:

Engineering analysis has determined that the structure is unstable and unsuited for its specified purpose of housing a "unicorn princess".

The implication that some engineer actually had to research the structural properties of cupcakes is priceless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Voynich manuscript was my single favourite part of this. Note that in the case of the researcher's daughter, it appeared as legible English, even though the scribblings were illegible; this acts as subtle confirmation that the Voynich manuscript is the real deal, somehow.

I also like the idea behind generic romantic novels being churned out as complete rubbish purely so the author can get a paycheck.

This isn't anything particularly noteworthy or horrifying, but tells a nice little story and is very well-written. Upvoting for execution rather than concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good lord. Well, thanks for the curse enlightenment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…

Wasn't this posted before? Is it a rewrite?

Never mind. Novote…

For now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Search TIME CUBE.

Laugh, then weep when you realize that the person behind Time Cube is serious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Trying… trying… trying…

…

…nope, can't seem to discern the meaning of this post. What the hell are you saying? :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YOU ARE EDUCATED STUPID. TIME CUBE 4/24 HOUR DAYS IS INCONTROVERTIBLE TRUTH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you've read the Night Watch books by Sergei Lukyanenko, the Twilight in those makes newspapers books have a similar effect. That's what came to mind as I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a rewrite, guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I seem to remember reading about something with similar if not identical effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Don't click if you want to figure it out yourself.
- From what I understand...

It seems that, when you view writing/text through it, it shows what the original person/persons who wrote it were thinking when they did write it.
"THIS IS A TEST" became "LET'S START WITH SOMETHING SIMPLE" because that's essentially what the researcher was thinking when they wrote it.

Of course, this could lead to some funny insight depending on who wrote it. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I'm totally getting deja-vu from that receipt for the cartload of wool, and the changed version of it. Like it's copy and pasted word for word. Probably from another scp that failed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still confused on what it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Totally comes across. And curse you, Voynich manuscript!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who recognises this? Has this been posted before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love these "guess the effect" ones; took me a second readthrough to figure this one out.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, ignore that past rendition of this comment. It's gone now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the things that bother me about 914 is the lack of detail on any test log. Even though it is are creative, i'd at least hope that there was something more for me to see about the object given the fact that it is a Series III entry. It fits the "thing that does something" cliché for me as well, and even though it is quite unique, i don't think i can vote just yet.

Like Metaphysician said, more exploration would really be nice to this entry. I'll wait and see where it goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now. Just not enough meat and would like to see the object explored more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So add more log entries. I'm opening this up as a collaborative, but I reserve the right to curate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is sound, and the logs are good, but what we learn is just so…ordinary. There was no "oh snap" moment, which I thought was coming after so many tests. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who thought that was a lightsaber? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe this is an absolutely excellent use of experiment logs and be something of an ideal example of what experiment log should hope to do. I do think that some of the examples could be cut (Zodiac killer, Voynich manuscript) however. It also isn't in the kind of style that fits my personal tastes.

I'm no-voting because of personal taste, but I'm still firmly of the thought that the logs are really well done structurally.
